children.heading: Environmental impact statement
children.label: 1 Environmental impact statement
children.text: The term “environmental impact statement” means the detailed statement of environmental impacts of a project required to be prepared pursuant to the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “environmental review process” means the process of preparing an environmental impact statement, environmental assessment, categorical exclusion, or other document under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) for a project study.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “environmental review process” includes the process for and completion of any environmental permit, approval, review, or study required for a project study under any Federal law other than the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Federal jurisdictional agency
children.label: 3 Federal jurisdictional agency
children.text: The term “Federal jurisdictional agency” means a Federal agency with jurisdiction delegated by law, regulation, order, or otherwise over a review, analysis, opinion, statement, permit, license, or other approval or decision required for a project study under applicable Federal laws (including regulations).

children.heading: Federal lead agency
children.label: 4 Federal lead agency
children.text: The term “Federal lead agency” means the Corps of Engineers.

children.heading: Project
children.label: 5 Project
children.text: The term “project” means a water resources development project to be carried out by the Secretary.

children.heading: Project sponsor
children.label: 6 Project sponsor
children.text: The term “project sponsor” has the meaning given the term “non-Federal interest” in .section 1962d–5b(b) of title 42

children.heading: Project study
children.label: 7 Project study
children.text: The term “project study” means a feasibility study for a project carried out pursuant to .section 2282 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall apply to each project study that is initiated after , and for which an environmental impact statement is prepared under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); andJune 10, 201442 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: B
children.text: may be applied, to the extent determined appropriate by the Secretary, to other project studies initiated after , and for which an environmental review process document is prepared under that Act.June 10, 2014

children.heading: Flexibility
children.label: 2 Flexibility
children.text: Any authority granted under this section may be exercised, and any requirement established under this section may be satisfied, for the conduct of an environmental review process for a project study, a class of project studies, or a program of project studies.

children.label: i
children.text: meets the standards described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not have adequate funding to make substantial progress toward the completion of the project study.

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The Secretary shall include for each project study on the list under subparagraph (A) a description of the estimated amounts necessary to make substantial progress on the project study.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement a coordinated environmental review process for the development of project studies.

children.heading: Coordinated review
children.label: 2 Coordinated review
children.text: The coordinated environmental review process described in paragraph (1) shall require that any review, analysis, opinion, statement, permit, license, or other approval or decision issued or made by a Federal, State, or local governmental agency or an Indian tribe for a project study described in subsection (b) be conducted, to the maximum extent practicable, concurrently with any other applicable governmental agency or Indian tribe.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 3 Timing
children.text: The coordinated environmental review process under this subsection shall be completed not later than the date on which the Secretary, in consultation and concurrence with the agencies identified under subsection (e), establishes with respect to the project study.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: At the discretion of the Secretary and subject to the requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and the requirements of section 1506.8 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations), including the concurrence of the proposed joint lead agency, a project sponsor may serve as the joint lead agency.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Project sponsor as joint lead agency
children.label: B Project sponsor as joint lead agency
i
children.text: with the concurrence of the Secretary, serve as a joint lead agency with the Federal lead agency for purposes of preparing any environmental document under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary provides guidance in the preparation process and independently evaluates that document;

children.label: aa
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: bb
children.text: any regulation implementing that Act; and

children.label: cc
children.text: any other applicable Federal law; and

children.label: III
children.text: the Secretary approves and adopts the document before the Secretary takes any subsequent action or makes any approval based on that document, regardless of whether the action or approval of the Secretary results in Federal funding.

children.label: A
children.text: the project sponsor complies with all design and mitigation commitments made jointly by the Secretary and the project sponsor in any environmental document prepared by the project sponsor in accordance with this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: any environmental document prepared by the project sponsor is appropriately supplemented to address any changes to the project the Secretary determines are necessary.

children.label: A
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: B
children.text: parts 1500 through 1508 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

children.heading: Roles and responsibility of lead agency
children.label: 4 Roles and responsibility of lead agency
A
children.text: to take such actions as are necessary and proper and within the authority of the Federal lead agency to facilitate the expeditious resolution of the environmental review process for the project study; and

children.label: B
children.text: to prepare or ensure that any required environmental impact statement or other environmental review document for a project study required to be completed under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) is completed in accordance with this section and applicable Federal law.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: A
children.text: have jurisdiction over the project;

children.label: B
children.text: be required by law to conduct or issue a review, analysis, opinion, or statement for the project study; or

children.label: C
children.text: be required to make a determination on issuing a permit, license, or other approval or decision for the project study.

children.label: A
children.text: have jurisdiction over the project;

children.label: B
children.text: are required to conduct or issue a review, analysis, opinion, or statement for the project study; or

children.label: C
children.text: are required to make a determination on issuing a permit, license, or other approval or decision for the project study.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal lead agency shall invite, as early as practicable in the environmental review process, any agency identified under paragraph (1) to become a participating or cooperating agency, as applicable, in the environmental review process for the project study.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: B Deadline
children.text: An invitation to participate issued under subparagraph (A) shall set a deadline by which a response to the invitation shall be submitted, which may be extended by the Federal lead agency for good cause.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: 4 Procedures
children.text: Section 1501.6 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ) shall govern the identification and the participation of a cooperating agency.June 10, 2014

children.label: I
children.text: has no jurisdiction or authority with respect to the project;

children.label: II
children.text: has no expertise or information relevant to the project; or

children.label: III
children.text: does not have adequate funds to participate in the project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not intend to submit comments on the project; or

children.label: B
children.text: does not intend to submit comments on the project.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 6 Administration
children.text: A participating or cooperating agency shall comply with this section and any schedule established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: supports a proposed project; or

children.label: B
children.text: has any jurisdiction over, or special expertise with respect to evaluation of, the project.

children.label: A
children.text: carry out the obligations of that agency under other applicable law concurrently and in conjunction with the required environmental review process, unless doing so would prevent the participating or cooperating agency from conducting needed analysis or otherwise carrying out those obligations; and

children.label: B
children.text: formulate and implement administrative, policy, and procedural mechanisms to enable the agency to ensure completion of the environmental review process in a timely, coordinated, and environmentally responsible manner.

children.label: A
children.text: eliminates repetitive discussions of the same issues;

children.label: B
children.text: focuses on the actual issues ripe for analyses at each level of review;

children.label: C
children.text: establishes a formal process for coordinating with participating and cooperating agencies, including the creation of a list of all data that is needed to carry out an environmental review process; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: ii
children.text: all other applicable laws.

children.label: A
children.text: as the first step in drafting guidance under that paragraph, consult with relevant Federal, State, and local governmental agencies, Indian tribes, and the public on the appropriate use and scope of the programmatic approaches;

children.label: B
children.text: emphasize the importance of collaboration among relevant Federal, State, and local governmental agencies, and Indian tribes in undertaking programmatic reviews, especially with respect to including reviews with a broad geographical scope;

children.label: i
children.text: promote transparency, including of the analyses and data used in the environmental review process, the treatment of any deferred issues raised by Federal, State, and local governmental agencies, Indian tribes, or the public, and the temporal and special scales to be used to analyze those issues;

children.label: I
children.text: criteria for determining the general duration of the usefulness of the review; and

children.label: II
children.text: the timeline for updating any out-of-date review;

children.label: I
children.text: the relationship between programmatic analysis and future tiered analysis; and

children.label: II
children.text: the role of the public in the creation of future tiered analysis; and

children.label: iv
children.text: are available to other relevant Federal, State, and local governmental agencies, Indian tribes, and the public;

children.label: D
children.text: allow not fewer than 60 days of public notice and comment on any proposed guidance; and

children.label: E
children.text: address any comments received under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Federal lead agency shall, after consultation with and with the concurrence of each participating and cooperating agency and the project sponsor or joint lead agency, as applicable, establish a plan for coordinating public and agency participation in, and comment on, the environmental review process for a project study or a category of project studies.

children.heading: Incorporation
children.label: ii Incorporation
children.text: The plan established under clause (i) shall be incorporated into the project schedule milestones set under .section 2282(g)(2) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: As soon as practicable but not later than 45 days after the close of the public comment period on a draft environmental impact statement, the Federal lead agency, after consultation with and the concurrence of each participating and cooperating agency and the project sponsor or joint lead agency, as applicable, shall establish, as part of the coordination plan established in subparagraph (A), a schedule for completion of the environmental review process for the project study.

children.label: I
children.text: the responsibilities of participating and cooperating agencies under applicable laws;

children.label: II
children.text: the resources available to the project sponsor, joint lead agency, and other relevant Federal and State agencies, as applicable;

children.label: III
children.text: the overall size and complexity of the project;

children.label: IV
children.text: the overall schedule for and cost of the project; and

children.label: V
children.text: the sensitivity of the natural and historical resources that could be affected by the project.

children.label: I
children.text: lengthen a schedule established under clause (i) for good cause; and

children.label: II
children.text: shorten a schedule only with concurrence of the affected participating and cooperating agencies and the project sponsor or joint lead agency, as applicable.

children.label: I
children.text: provided to each participating and cooperating agency and the project sponsor or joint lead agency, as applicable; and

children.label: II
children.text: made available to the public.

children.label: i
children.text: a different deadline is established by agreement of the Federal lead agency, the project sponsor or joint lead agency, as applicable, and all participating and cooperating agencies; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the deadline is extended by the Federal lead agency for good cause.

children.label: i
children.text: a different deadline is established by agreement of the Federal lead agency, the project sponsor, or joint lead agency, as applicable, and all participating and cooperating agencies; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the deadline is extended by the Federal lead agency for good cause.

children.heading: Deadlines for decisions under other laws
children.label: 3 Deadlines for decisions under other laws
A
children.text: as soon as practicable after the 180-day period described in subsection (h)(5)(B)(ii), an initial notice of the failure of the Federal agency to make the decision; and

children.label: B
children.text: every 60 days thereafter until such date as all decisions of the Federal agency relating to the project study have been made by the Federal agency, an additional notice that describes the number of decisions of the Federal agency that remain outstanding as of the date of the additional notice.

children.heading: Involvement of the public
children.label: 4 Involvement of the public
children.text: Nothing in this subsection reduces any time period provided for public comment in the environmental review process under applicable Federal law (including regulations).

children.heading: Reporting requirements
children.label: A Reporting requirements
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish and maintain an electronic database and, in coordination with other Federal and State agencies, issue reporting requirements to make publicly available the status and progress with respect to compliance with applicable requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et. seq.) and any other Federal, State, or local approval or action required for a project study for which this section is applicable.June 10, 201442 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Project study transparency
children.label: B Project study transparency
children.text: Consistent with the requirements established under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall publish the status and progress of any Federal, State, or local decision, action, or approval required under applicable laws for each project study for which this section is applicable.

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: 1 Cooperation
children.text: The Federal lead agency, the cooperating agencies, and any participating agencies shall work cooperatively in accordance with this section to identify and resolve issues that could delay completion of the environmental review process or result in the denial of any approval required for the project study under applicable laws.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal lead agency shall make information available to the cooperating agencies and participating agencies as early as practicable in the environmental review process regarding the environmental and socioeconomic resources located within the project area and the general locations of the alternatives under consideration.

children.heading: Data sources
children.label: B Data sources
children.text: The information under subparagraph (A) may be based on existing data sources, including geographic information systems mapping.

children.heading: Cooperating and participating agency responsibilities
children.label: 3 Cooperating and participating agency responsibilities
children.text: Based on information received from the Federal lead agency, cooperating and participating agencies shall identify, as early as practicable, any issues of concern regarding the potential environmental or socioeconomic impacts of the project, including any issues that could substantially delay or prevent an agency from granting a permit or other approval that is needed for the project study.

children.label: i
children.text: delay completion of the environmental review process; or

children.label: ii
children.text: result in denial of any approval required for the project study under applicable laws.

children.heading: Meeting date
children.label: B Meeting date
children.text: A meeting requested under this paragraph shall be held not later than 21 days after the date on which the Secretary receives the request for the meeting, unless the Secretary determines that there is good cause to extend that deadline.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: C Notification
children.text: On receipt of a request for a meeting under this paragraph, the Secretary shall notify all relevant participating and cooperating agencies of the request, including the issue to be resolved and the date for the meeting.

children.heading: Elevation of issue resolution
children.label: D Elevation of issue resolution
children.text: If a resolution cannot be achieved within the 30 day-period beginning on the date of a meeting under this paragraph and a determination is made by the Secretary that all information necessary to resolve the issue has been obtained, the Secretary shall forward the dispute to the heads of the relevant agencies for resolution.

children.heading: Convention by Secretary
children.label: E Convention by Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may convene an issue resolution meeting under this paragraph at any time, at the discretion of the Secretary, regardless of whether a meeting is requested under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A Federal jurisdictional agency shall complete any required approval or decision for the environmental review process on an expeditious basis using the shortest existing applicable process.

children.label: I
children.text: $20,000 for any project study requiring the preparation of an environmental assessment or environmental impact statement; or

children.label: II
children.text: $10,000 for any project study requiring any type of review under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) other than an environmental assessment or environmental impact statement.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: I
children.text: the date that is 180 days after the date on which an application for the permit, license, or approval is complete; and

children.label: II
children.text: the date that is 180 days after the date on which the Federal lead agency issues a decision on the project under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No transfer of funds under subparagraph (B) relating to an individual project study shall exceed, in any fiscal year, an amount equal to 1 percent of the funds made available for the applicable agency office.

children.heading: Failure to decide
children.label: ii Failure to decide
children.text: The total amount transferred in a fiscal year as a result of a failure by an agency to make a decision by an applicable deadline shall not exceed an amount equal to 5 percent of the funds made available for the applicable agency office for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Aggregate
children.label: iii Aggregate
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for each fiscal year, the aggregate amount of financial penalties assessed against each applicable agency office under the Water Resources Reform and Development Act of 2014 and any other Federal law as a result of a failure of the agency to make a decision by an applicable deadline for environmental review, including the total amount transferred under this paragraph, shall not exceed an amount equal to 9.5 percent of the funds made available for the agency office for that fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: the agency has not received necessary information or approvals from another entity in a manner that affects the ability of the agency to meet any requirements under Federal, State, or local law;

children.label: II
children.text: significant new information, including from public comments, or circumstances, including a major modification to an aspect of the project, requires additional analysis for the agency to make a decision on the project application; or

children.label: III
children.text: the agency lacks the financial resources to complete the review under the scheduled time frame, including a description of the number of full-time employees required to complete the review, the amount of funding required to complete the review, and a justification as to why not enough funding is available to complete the review by the deadline.

children.label: I
children.text: conduct a financial audit to review the notice; and

children.label: II
children.text: not later than 90 days after the date on which the review described in subclause (I) is completed, submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on the notice.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: E Limitation
children.text: The Federal agency from which funds are transferred pursuant to this paragraph shall not reprogram funds to the office of the head of the agency, or equivalent office, to reimburse that office for the loss of the funds.

children.heading: Effect of paragraph
children.label: F Effect of paragraph
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph affects or limits the application of, or obligation to comply with, any Federal, State, local, or tribal law.

children.heading: Memorandum of agreements for early coordination
children.label: i Memorandum of agreements for early coordination
A
children.text: the Secretary and other Federal agencies with relevant jurisdiction in the environmental review process should cooperate with each other, State agencies, and Indian tribes on environmental review and project delivery activities at the earliest practicable time to avoid delays and duplication of effort later in the process, prevent potential conflicts, and ensure that planning and project development decisions reflect environmental values; and

children.label: B
children.text: the cooperation referred to in subparagraph (A) should include the development of policies and the designation of staff that advise planning agencies and project sponsors of studies or other information foreseeably required for later Federal action and early consultation with appropriate State and local agencies and Indian tribes.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 2 Technical assistance
children.text: If requested at any time by a State or project sponsor, the Secretary and other Federal agencies with relevant jurisdiction in the environmental review process, shall, to the maximum extent practicable and appropriate, as determined by the agencies, provide technical assistance to the State or project sponsor in carrying out early coordination activities.

children.heading: Memorandum of agency agreement
children.label: 3 Memorandum of agency agreement
children.text: If requested at any time by a State or project sponsor, the Federal lead agency, in consultation with other Federal agencies with relevant jurisdiction in the environmental review process, may establish memoranda of agreement with the project sponsor, Indian tribe, State and local governments, and other appropriate entities to carry out the early coordination activities, including providing technical assistance in identifying potential impacts and mitigation issues in an integrated fashion.

children.label: A
children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: B
children.text: any other Federal environmental law;
the reviewability of any final Federal agency action in a court of the United States or in the court of any State;
any requirement for seeking, considering, or responding to public comment; or

any power, jurisdiction, responsibility, duty, or authority that a Federal, State, or local governmental agency, Indian tribe, or project sponsor has with respect to carrying out a project or any other provision of law applicable to projects.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a claim arising under Federal law seeking judicial review of a permit, license, or other approval issued by a Federal agency for a project study shall be barred unless the claim is filed not later than 3 years after publication of a notice in the Federal Register announcing that the permit, license, or other approval is final pursuant to the law under which the agency action is taken, unless a shorter time is specified in the Federal law that allows judicial review.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: Nothing in this subsection creates a right to judicial review or places any limit on filing a claim that a person has violated the terms of a permit, license, or other approval.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall consider new information received after the close of a comment period if the information satisfies the requirements for a supplemental environmental impact statement under title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (including successor regulations).

children.heading: Separate action
children.label: B Separate action
children.text: The preparation of a supplemental environmental impact statement or other environmental document, if required under this section, shall be considered a separate final agency action and the deadline for filing a claim for judicial review of the action shall be 3 years after the date of publication of a notice in the Federal Register announcing the action relating to such supplemental environmental impact statement or other environmental document.

children.label: A
children.text: survey the use by the Corps of Engineers of categorical exclusions in projects since 2005;

children.label: i
children.text: the types of actions that were categorically excluded or could be the basis for developing a new categorical exclusion; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any requests previously received by the Secretary for new categorical exclusions; and

children.label: C
children.text: solicit requests from other Federal agencies and project sponsors for new categorical exclusions.

children.heading: New categorical exclusions
children.label: 2 New categorical exclusions
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , if the Secretary has identified a category of activities that merit establishing a categorical exclusion that did not exist on the day before , based on the review under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall publish a notice of proposed rulemaking to propose that new categorical exclusion, to the extent that the categorical exclusion meets the criteria for a categorical exclusion under section 1508.4 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulation).June 10, 2014June 10, 2014

children.heading: Review of project acceleration reforms
children.label: m Review of project acceleration reforms
A
children.text: assess the reforms carried out under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 5 years and not later than 10 years after , submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report that describes the results of the assessment.June 10, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: project delivery;

children.label: B
children.text: compliance with environmental laws; and

children.label: C
children.text: the environmental impact of projects.

children.heading: Performance measurement
children.label: n Performance measurement
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to measure and report on progress made toward improving and expediting the planning and environmental review process.

children.heading: Implementation guidance
children.label: o Implementation guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall prepare, in consultation with the Council on Environmental Quality and other Federal agencies with jurisdiction over actions or resources that may be impacted by a project, guidance documents that describe the coordinated environmental review processes that the Secretary intends to use to implement this section for the planning of projects, in accordance with the civil works program of the Corps of Engineers and all applicable law.
